messages.content: Build a hand-rolled fp-ts Lens<User, string> focused on user.address.city from scratch. Use the standalone pipe to set a new city without mutation, and expose a compose helper that zooms two Lenses together.
What's the fp-ts way to update a deeply nested field without mutation? Show a minimal hand-rolled Lens example.
Explain briefly how a Lens helps with immutable nested updates in fp-ts, then show a working typed implementation that bumps a counter in a nested settings record.
Write a hand-rolled fp-ts Lens module. Constraints: no any, one generic `prop` factory, `modify` helper, and a `compose` that never mutates input.

Build a hand-rolled fp-ts Optional<S, A> from scratch. The `getOption` must return Option<A> and `set` must leave the structure intact when the focus is absent. Demonstrate it on a user whose middleName field is optional.
What's the fp-ts way to safely read and update a possibly-missing nested field? Show a minimal Optional example.
Explain briefly the difference between a Lens and an Optional in fp-ts, then show a working typed Optional that focuses on an array's first element.
Write an Optional that focuses the value of a key in a Record<string, V>. Constraints: no any, use O.fromNullable, and set must not create the key if absent.
Build an Optional that drills into union-shaped state: given `State = {kind:'loaded',data:X} | {kind:'loading'}`, focus X when kind is 'loaded'. Show read/set/modify on both variants.
Build a hand-rolled Traversal<S, A> that can map over many foci at once. Apply it to bump every price in a readonly array of cart items.
What's the fp-ts way to apply the same transformation across every element of a nested array without writing map-of-map? Show a minimal Traversal example.
Explain briefly when you'd reach for a Traversal over a Lens, then show a working typed Traversal that uppercases every tag in an array of posts.
Write a Traversal module. Constraints: no any, provide an `of` constructor, `compose` between Traversals, and demonstrate composing array-of-array-of-number.
Build a Traversal that focuses on every 'approved' item's score in a nested object `{pending:Item[],approved:Item[]}`. Apply a 10-point bonus to each approved score.
Build a hand-rolled fp-ts Prism<S, A> for the 'Left' branch of a tagged union `Result = {tag:'Left', e:string} | {tag:'Right', a:number}`. Demonstrate getOption and reverseGet.
What's the fp-ts way to isomorphically convert between a tuple and an object without losing type info? Show a minimal Iso example.
Explain briefly how a Prism differs from a Lens, then show a working typed Prism for the success branch of `Result<E,A>`.
Write a Prism-based validator that accepts only positive numbers wrapped as `{tag:'Pos',n:number}`. Constraints: no any, compose `getOption` with O.chain so invalid values are dropped.

Refactor this ad-hoc branch check to use a fp-ts Prism so the success branch is extracted cleanly: ```ts
const val = r._tag==='Ok' ? r.value : 0
```
Build a pipeline that converts a Record<string, number> to an array of [k, v] pairs, filters values > 10, maps them to uppercase keys, and rebuilds a Record. Use fp-ts/Record, fp-ts/Array, and pipe.
What's the fp-ts way to transform a Record by filtering entries with a predicate over both key and value? Show a minimal round-trip via Array.
Explain briefly why converting Record -> Array -> Record is a common fp-ts idiom, then show a working typed example that increments every value by 1.
Write a Record-grouping pipeline. Constraints: input ReadonlyArray<User>, group by role into Record<string, User[]>, no any, use pipe + A.reduce.

Add a sort-by-value step before rebuilding the Record so keys come back in descending order of value: ```ts
pipe(rec, R.toEntries, R.fromEntries)
```

Fix this to use fp-ts Record+Array instead of a manual for-loop: ```ts
const out:Record<string,number>={};for(const k in r) if(r[k]>0) out[k]=r[k]*2
```
Build an Effect v3 Match.type handler for a discriminated union `Event = Click | KeyDown | Focus` (all with a `_tag` field). Each branch returns a distinct Effect.succeed string.
What's the Effect v3 way to exhaustively pattern match on a tagged union inside an Effect program? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Match.value differs from Match.type, then show a working typed example that matches on a runtime value against literal strings.
Write an Effect v3 command dispatcher. Constraints: use Match.type, three tags (Start, Stop, Report), each branch returns Effect.succeed with a description, exhaustive check.

Add a new `Cancel` branch to this Effect v3 Match dispatcher: ```ts
type Cmd={_tag:'Run'}|{_tag:'Skip'}
Match.type<Cmd>()...Match.exhaustive
```
Build an Effect v3 Match dispatcher that converts an HTTP-shaped union `{_tag:'Ok',body:string}|{_tag:'NotFound'}|{_tag:'Err',code:number}` into a Effect<string, Error>. On 'Err', fail with an Error.
Build an Effect v3 Match pipeline that classifies a number with predicate guards: negative, zero, positive-small (<10), positive-big (>=10). Each branch returns Effect.succeed with a label.
What's the Effect v3 way to dispatch on a predicate rather than a tag? Show a minimal Match.when example.
Explain briefly how Match.when lets you guard a branch with a refinement, then show a working typed example that narrows `unknown` to `string` via typeof.
Write an Effect v3 Match.when router. Constraints: three predicate branches (isAdmin, isOwner, isMember), each returns Effect.succeed with a permission string, fall-through to 'none'.
